title: Record Management on SEQ
text: Record Management on SEQ The table shows the data limits imposed by the SEQ connector: Table 1. SEQ Connector Data Limits Limits allowed Amount Fields per record Not applicable: maximum record length = 8192 bytes Length of record 8192 bytes Fields per key Not applicable Amount of indexes Not applicable Maximum length of key Not applicable Maximum length of a field Not applicable: maximum record length = 8192 bytes File Creation Files are created on the fly at run time. File names and field names must comply with Uniface naming conventions.Your file names must also comply with the rules of the operating system. Primary key fields are reproduced in a file of the same name with the prefix O . Primary Keys All storage formats are allowed for key fields. However, it is not recommended that you use certain storage formats (Boolean, for example) for key fields. If a sequential file contains a compound primary key, the primary key fields must be contiguous. If the primary key fields are not contiguous, Uniface is unable to convert in either direction and returns the warning data fetch error . Indexes Sequential files do not have indexes. The SEQ connector maintains indexes internally (that is, in the connector) as soon as a file is accessed. These indexes are maintained on the basis of the conceptual definitions for that file. The SEQ connector cannot create indexes in sequential files. Related Links Naming Rules for Uniface Development Objects The name of a development object is its unique identifier. When naming development objects, use the allowed syntax for the object name. Avoid using words that are reserved for use within Uniface and your own environment, including the operating system and database. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
